pr.title: Match 22 functions across stage, fighter, menu, match-end, and synth TUs
pr.author: davidfeira

summary: Matched 22 previously stubbed or partial functions across stage, fighter, menu, match-end/game-mode, vi demo, and sysdolphin synth translation units. The PR converted several UNK prototypes into typed signatures, added small file-local layout structs where needed for data access, and verified the work with a full ninja SHA1 match plus per-function objdiff 100% reports. A temporary adjacent-TU regression from a cherry-picked dont_inline pragma was identified and removed before merge.
what_changed: Replaced function stubs with matched C implementations for 22 functions across 13 TUs: gricemt, grhomerun, granime, ftCo_Attack100, ftKb_Init, ftKb_SpecialN, ftKb_SpecialNYs, mndatadel, mnmainrule, gm_1601, gm_1BA8, vi1101, and synth.
Implemented stage animation and stage-control functions including grAnime_801C8138, Ice Mountain state/scroll helpers, and Home-Run Contest stage/text setup.

Implemented fighter-side behavior for common throw-release effects, Kirby Special-N collision impact effects, Kirby Special-Lw collision/release state initialization, and Kirby/Yoshi egg shrink scaling.
Implemented menu and scene functions for Data Delete warning dialog animation/input, main-rule snapshot collection, and vi1101 demo character setup.

Implemented match-end and game-mode functions including loser/tiebreak reducers, persistent KO counter accumulation, tournament-event player initialization, event end-of-round arbitration, and fn_80169F50 in gm_1601.c as shown in the diff excerpt.
Implemented sysdolphin synth stream-prefetch logic including HSD_Synth_8038B120 and HSD_SynthPStreamHeaderCallback.

Refined headers by replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or void placeholders with typed prototypes for grIceMt_801FA364, mn_80230D18, un_8031F294, ftKb_SpecialN_800F1DAC, fn_80161004, fn_80162068, fn_80161C90, grHomeRun_8021EC58, and mnDataDel_8024EBC8.
Added a file-local struct ftKb_Init_803CB490_layout in ftKb_SpecialN.c to access a Vec3 at offset 0x74 from ftKb_Init_803CB490 for Kirby rock fragment initialization.
smart_moves: Used typed prototypes to make call sites and bodies agree, such as changing ftKb_SpecialN_800F1DAC from an unknown signature to void ftKb_SpecialN_800F1DAC(HSD_GObj* gobj) and updating the caller to pass gobj.
Kept uncertain data layout local when only a small slice was needed: ftKb_Init_803CB490_layout pads to offset 0x74 and exposes only the Vec3 required by the matched Kirby Special-Lw code.
Handled collision-effect edge detection by comparing current env_flags against prev_env_flags, avoiding repeated particle spawns and matching the intended rising-edge behavior.
Preserved subtle state during Kirby Special-Lw ground transition by saving/restoring x221C_b4 across Fighter_ChangeMotionState and then setting animation rate and damage/death callbacks.
Used explicit saturation to 0xFFFF when accumulating persistent fighter KO counts, matching save/stat behavior without overflowing the u16 destination.

Validated that broad multi-TU matching did not damage nearby functions: an initial CI/objdiff regression caused by an unrelated dont_inline pragma was isolated, removed, and the adjacent function restored to 100%.
decomp_lessons: For multi-function matching PRs, update headers alongside bodies; unknown prototypes can hide argument-passing and return-type details that are required for both matching and future call-site cleanup.
When a global data blob's full structure is not known, a minimal file-local layout with padding to the accessed field can be an acceptable bridge if the evidence is limited to that field.

Objdiff regressions in adjacent functions may come from pragmas or matching tactics carried over from unrelated partial work; remove such tactics if they improve one future target but reduce current matched code.

Stage animation code often needs to coordinate JObj hierarchy traversal, archive lookups, joint/material/shape anim arrays, and optional AObj flag setup; treating these as parallel arrays helped match grAnime_801C8138.

Game-mode and match-end functions often encode active/inactive slots with sentinel values or slot_type checks; typed structs such as MatchEnd, MatchPlayerData, and MatchTeamData make the control flow easier to preserve.
Repeated initialization patterns, such as copying the same Vec3 into multiple movement-state slots and zeroing paired scratch arrays, should be kept explicit when that is what the compiler expects.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based names for still-unknown or internal functions such as fn_800D84D4, fn_8010AA64, fn_80161004, fn_80162068, fn_801661E0, fn_80169F50, and un_8031F294.

Used subsystem-prefixed names where existing naming was known: grAnime_801C8138, grIceMt_801FA364, grHomeRun_8021EC58, mnDataDel_8024EBC8, gm_801BAB40, gm_801BD46C, and HSD_SynthPStreamHeaderCallback.
Header refinements prefer concrete project types over UNK placeholders, for example MatchEnd*, HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, HSD_Text*, Vec3, s32, u8, and bool.
Temporary local layout names were scoped to the function/data they describe, e.g. struct ftKb_Init_803CB490_layout and gm_801BAB40_src.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations in matched functions to reproduce stack layout where needed, including fighter, game-mode, and synth functions.
Matched float/vector initialization by keeping explicit field assignments and repeated Vec3 copies instead of abstracting into helper loops.
Used casts to access archive/data fields at known offsets when full types were incomplete, such as animation archive arrays and the ftKb_Init_803CB490 Vec3 layout.
Preserved branch structure for effect dispatch and event-ending logic rather than deduplicating repeated cleanup blocks, which can be important for matching generated code.
Verified each function with objdiff-cli at 100.0% and performed a full ninja build producing a matching main.dol SHA1.
Removed an unrelated dont_inline pragma after it caused an adjacent function fn_800DAD18 to regress from 100% to 90.31%, restoring the match instead of accepting a fuzzy regression.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.

The PR body records a CI/matching feedback loop: an initial fuzzy regression in fn_800DAD18 was traced to a cherry-picked dont_inline pragma from partial ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys work; the pragma was removed, restoring fn_800DAD18 to 100% while leaving ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys at upstream's existing partial match.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace ftKb_Init_803CB490_layout with a full typed struct.
Check whether fn_80169F50 was included in the counted 22 functions despite not being highlighted in the summary list.
Look for follow-up matches for ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys after the dont_inline pragma was removed.
Search for additional header cleanup around gm_1601 MatchEnd and MatchPlayerData after PR 2462.
Search for later synth.c matches building on HSD_Synth_8038B120 and HSD_SynthPStreamHeaderCallback.
